The entire word of God is useful to direct us in prayer; but here, as often elsewhere, the Holy Spirit has put words into our mouths. Here is a prayer placed in the hands of the afflicted; let them present it to God. Even good men may be almost overwhelmed with afflictions. It is our duty and interest to pray; and it is comfort to an afflicted spirit to unburden itself by a humble representation of its griefs. We must say, Blessed be the name of the Lord, who both gives and takes away. The psalmist looked upon himself as a dying man; My days are like a shadow.

We are dying creatures, but God is an everlasting God, the protector of his church; we may be confident that it will not be neglected. When we consider our own vileness, our darkness and deadness, and the many defects in our prayers, we have cause to fear that they will not be received in heaven; but we are here assured of the contrary, for we have an Advocate with the Father, and are under grace, not under the law. Redemption is the subject of praise in the Christian church; and that great work is described by the temporal deliverance and restoration of Israel. Look down upon us, Lord Jesus; and bring us into the glorious liberty of your children, that we may bless and praise your name.

Bodily illnesses soon weaken our strength, so what can we expect but that our lives should be cut off in the midst? And what should we do but prepare accordingly? We must acknowledge God’s hand in it; and reconcile this to his love, for often those who have used their strength well have it weakened; and those who, as we think, can very ill be spared, have their days shortened. It is very comforting, with respect to all the changes and dangers of the church, to remember that Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever. And with respect to the death of our bodies, and the removal of friends, to remember that God is an everlasting God. Do not let us overlook the assurance this psalm contains of a happy end to all the believer’s trials. Though all things are changing, dying, perishing, like clothing folding up and hastening to decay, yet Jesus lives, and thus all is secure, for he has said, Because I live you shall live also.
